senses.definition: a wrestler who is a bad guy or has a villainous character, typically followed by the audience dislikes and boos

examples.x: The heel insulted his opponent's hometown to rile up the audience.
The heel taunted the crowd after his cheap victory.
Despite being a heel, he had a strong fan following for his charisma.
The heel's manager interfered in the match to help him win unfairly.
Fans threw trash into the ring to express their disdain for the heel.

senses.definition: a heroic or good-natured character in wrestling, typically loved by the audience

examples.x: The face of the wrestling world, John Cena, always fights for justice and honor.
The crowd erupted in cheers as their favorite face entered the ring.
Being a face means embodying virtues like courage and integrity.
Faces engage with the audience, rallying them behind their cause.
The face's entrance music energizes the crowd before each match.

senses.definition: (in boxing) an athlete that weighs between 59 and 61.2 kg and competes in lightweight class
a boxer who weighs more than 91kg and competes in heavyweight class
a boxer who competes in the weight class that ranges from 70 to 73 kilograms

examples.x: The middleweight showcased his agility and power in the ring.

His speed and agility make him a formidable middleweight.
Her coach praises her as a middleweight with exceptional footwork.

The light heavyweight's endurance and strength were key to his victory in the championship bout.
Every light heavyweight must maintain a strict diet and training regimen to stay within their weight class.

senses.definition: a boxer who defensively reacts to opponents' attacks with precise punches
